summary: A small two-story rectangular house with a curved gable-style brick roof, continuous horizontal window bands on both floors, and a low white stone border platform around the base. Walls are layered with a light sand-colored block and a wooden plank trim between floors, with darker corner pillars.

shape.overall_form: compact rectangular block with a stepped curved roof (rounded gable)
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric along the long axis (left-right symmetry visible from front and top)

elements: continuous window bands on both floors (three or more panes wide)
stepped curved brick roof with quartz/stone edging
wooden plank horizontal trim between floors
low white stone/quartz border platform around building
rebuild_hints: Use a rectangular footprint roughly 11x7 blocks; place a one-block high white/quartz border platform inset from the edge by 1 block as shown.

Walls: two stacked stories of sandstone-like blocks with a continuous horizontal wooden plank layer between stories; add glass pane windows as continuous horizontal strips (approximately 3-4 windows per visible face).
Corners: place darker stonebrick blocks as vertical corner pillars spanning both floors.
Roof: build a symmetrical stepped curved gable using brick blocks centered on the long axis; cap edges with quartz/stone slabs to match the light edging.
Height: each floor about 3 blocks tall; roof rises ~2-3 blocks above second floor in stepped layers.
uncertainties: Exact block types: sandstone vs smooth_sandstone or cut variants uncertain; I labeled as sandstone.
Base border material could be quartz or smooth_stone (confidence 0.8).
Corner pillar material looks like stonebrick or polished_andesite; uncertain (confidence 0.7).
Exact internal layout and presence/location of doors are not visible in provided views.
Precise footprint dimensions inferred from visible window counts and roof width; could be off by a few blocks.
